summary: This whitepaper describes VitaDAO's funding of the Korolchuk Lab at Newcastle University to discover novel autophagy activators for age-related diseases through a blockchain-based IP-NFT and token sale mechanism. The project focuses on screening ~4000 bioactive compounds to identify autophagy activators targeting lysosomal dysfunction in diseases like Niemann-Pick Type C, with potential broader applications for Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.

content_blocks.title: Executive Summary
content_blocks.content: VitaDAO is funding longevity research by Viktor Korolchuk lab at Newcastle University through sale of VITA-FAST tokens (ERC-20 tokens representing fractions of an IP-NFT). Tokens confer governance rights over IP generated by the research. The project aims to discover novel autophagy activators. Funding mechanism uses blockchain/cryptocurrency technology. VITA-FAST token issued in compliance with Swiss law.

content_blocks.title: Project Overview - Research Focus
content_blocks.content: VitaDAO owns IP rights to research on autophagy activators by Korolchuk Lab at Newcastle University. Lab led by Dr. Viktor Korolchuk with extensive experience in autophagy and lysosomal biology. Research focuses on molecular mechanisms controlling cellular homeostasis and aging to identify therapeutic targets for age-related diseases. Lab affiliated with Newcastle University with international collaborations. VitaDAO initially supported via Sponsored Research Agreement (SRA) to fund investigations into cellular mechanisms of aging and drug discovery.

content_blocks.title: Project Background - Autophagy Biology
content_blocks.content: Aging is associated with decline in autophagy capacity. Autophagy activation considered promising therapeutic approach for aging and age-related diseases. Autophagy is the cell's waste-management system for sequestering, degrading, and recycling faulty cellular components (dysfunctional organelles, misfolded proteins, defective DNA, reactive oxygen species). Impaired autophagy strongly linked to neurodegenerative disorders: Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease, and ALS.

content_blocks.title: Project Background - Lysosomal Focus
content_blocks.content: Project focuses on autophagy activators with specific attention to the lysosome (the vesicle carrying degradation enzymes). Alzheimer's and Parkinson's have high incidence of lysosomal dysfunction. Disease model: Niemann-Pick Type C (NPC) chosen as monogenetic orphan disease with well-understood biological causes, providing shorter path to drug approval. Once approved for NPC, potential to expand to other lysosomal storage disorders and diseases with lysosomal dysfunction (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's).

content_blocks.title: Mechanism of Faulty Autophagy Leading to Cell Death
content_blocks.content: Dr. Korolchuk previously contributed to establishing the mechanism: (1) Dysfunctional mitochondria accumulate due to inadequate recycling from faulty autophagy, (2) Accumulation of ROS and DNA damage induces cellular stress, (3) Stress response pathways activated (PARP & SIRT), (4) Depletion of NAD+ and NADH essential to cellular metabolism, (5) Mitochondria lose ability to generate ATP as they become depolarized, (6) Apoptosis (programmed cell death) begins.

content_blocks.title: Experimental Model and Assay Design
content_blocks.content: Model system: cells with mutation in lysosomal protein Niemann-Pick C1 (Npc1 -/-) associated with neurodegenerative diseases. When subjected to metabolic stress, these cells suffer cell death due to dysfunctional autophagy, providing readout for autophagy assay (cells dead/cells alive). Control: cells lacking autophagy initiation (Atg5 -/-) are not rescuable by autophagy inducers. By testing compounds in both Atg5 -/- and Npc1 -/-, can differentiate between compounds that specifically activate autophagy (successful in Npc1-/-) versus compounds inducing cell survival unrelated to autophagy (successful in Atg5-/-).

content_blocks.title: Experimental Plan - Screening and Validation
content_blocks.content: Screening ~4000 bioactive molecules and commercial small molecules. Library includes unique plant and animal compounds from KVP lab with bioavailability useful for drug translation. Experimental steps: (1) Assay optimization and compound screening in cell survival assays (Atg5-/- vs Npc1-/-) with dose-response effects using mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs), (2) Luciferase-p62 clearance assay, (3) Halo orthogonal assay, (4) Selection and purchase of derivatives of lead series, (5) Screening commercial derivatives in cell survival, p62 clearance, and Halo assay, (6) Synthesis of lead series at Contract Research Organisation (CRO) and determination of mechanism of action, (7) Structure-activity relationship (SAR) and validation in vitro autophagy assays.

content_blocks.title: Cell Survival Assay Details
content_blocks.content: Bioactive molecules and commercial small molecules screened in cell survival assay to find molecules that reactivate autophagy in NPC1 -/- cells. Assay ensures all molecules moved to further screening are not cytotoxic, therefore suitable for therapeutic development.

content_blocks.title: Luciferase-p62 Clearance Assay
content_blocks.content: Generation and optimization of Npc1 -/- MEFs with inducible tet-on-tet-off expression of luciferase-tagged autophagy flux reporter p62. p62 protein clearance only possible with proper autophagy. By quantifying clearance, can confirm autophagy is occurring and measure level of activity.

content_blocks.title: Halo-GFP-LC3 Orthogonal Assay
content_blocks.content: Generation and optimization of Npc1 -/- cells stably expressing 'traffic-light' EGFP-RFP-LC3 for high throughput high content screening. Automated image analysis provides information about: number of autophagosomes, number of autolysosomes, and whether autophagy has been activated.

content_blocks.title: Project Status - Current Progress
content_blocks.content: To date: Dr. Korolchuk and team have generated data on thousands of compounds from initial library using described assays. Several compounds emerged with potential autophagic properties. Lead series identified, library of derivatives classified, and synthesized by external suppliers. Small molecules screened have high chemical variability and no similarity to pre-existing autophagy inducers, representing high possibility of novel IP. At time of publishing: screening second generation of compounds developed as lead series based on hits, waiting on delivery of additional chemical series. These derivatives will undergo same screening process (cell survival, p62 clearance, autolysosome quantification).

content_blocks.title: Future Plans - Drug Translation
content_blocks.content: Once lead molecules identified, VitaDAO and Molecule will work with Newcastle University and Korolchuk lab on drug translation process. Will need to consider: pharmacokinetics, target deconvolution, structure-activity relationships, scalability, and manufacturing.

content_blocks.title: Future Plans - Further Research Models
content_blocks.content: Upon completion, further research in in vitro and in vivo models needed. Possibilities include: (1) Niemann-Pick Type C patient-derived fibroblasts or CRISPR-Cas9 Npc1 knock-out cells to examine efficacy in human cell model, (2) Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) to model disease progression in different cell types, (3) Npc1-mutant mice and cats displaying same symptoms as humans with Niemann-Pick Type C provide strong in vivo models to evaluate neurological outcomes and potential toxicity in large animal models.

content_blocks.title: Future Plans - Clinical Development Pathway
content_blocks.content: Clinical studies pathway: Phase 1 Clinical Trials (20-100 healthy volunteers/patients to evaluate safety, dosage, side effects), Phase 2 Clinical Trials (for Niemann-Pick Type C, Orphan Drug designation could be sought providing benefits including tax credits and waiver of FDA user fee), Phase 3 Clinical Trials (if successful in NPC, could be tried for Alzheimer's disease as future indication requiring full separate clinical trials), FDA Review and Market (New Drug Application submitted after Phase 3, FDA review takes up to 2 years but may be faster for drugs with Breakthrough Therapy designation).

content_blocks.title: Blockchain/NFT Technology Introduction
content_blocks.content: Blockchain is decentralized digital ledger technology for secure transparent record-keeping. NFTs (Non-fungible tokens) use blockchain to verify ownership, uniqueness, and provenance of digital assets. IP-NFTs are specific NFTs representing intellectual property and data rights to scientific research, attaching legal contracts to smart contracts. Can be transferred peer-to-peer, made composable with DeFi, used to distribute governance to stakeholders. First IP-NFTs minted by Molecule for VitaDAO to register longevity therapeutics IP and R&D data rights on Ethereum.

content_blocks.title: Tokenomics - Rights of VITA-FAST Token Holders
content_blocks.content: VITA-FAST tokens represent membership in IP pool containing Korolchuk IP-NFT. Rights governed by Korolchuk-Free Association of Molecules (FAM) Membership Agreement: (1) Governance - right to participate in governance of IP-NFT development including voting on proposed licenses and use of proceeds, (2) Access to Intellectual Property - access to relevant data and IP arising from development for due diligence and informed governance (does not grant license to IP), (3) Duty of Care - must honor, support, adhere to terms of FAM Membership Agreement, (4) Confidentiality - must not use or disclose confidential information or IP without consent, cannot sell or register Confidential Information, IP, or R&D data.

content_blocks.title: Token Distribution
content_blocks.content: Total 1,000,000 VITA-FAST tokens minted. Distribution: 10% (100,000 tokens) sold to max 499 VITA tokenholders with 2-month vesting + 2-month cliff; 69% retained by VitaDAO; 5% to Researchers vested over 4 years with 1-year cliff (incentivizes long-term success, discourages data falsification); 1% to Newcastle University vested over 4 years with 1-year cliff (signal to other University TTOs, incentive to speed licensing); 5% to VitaDAO dealflow working group contributors vested over 4 years with 1-year cliff (subject to VitaDAO governance approval); 10% to liquidity pool (Uniswap V3 pool matching 10% tokens with 10% funds raised).

content_blocks.title: Token Distribution Pie Chart
content_blocks.content: Visual representation showing: VitaDAO (liquid) 69%, Genesis Sale (liquid) 10%, Liquidity Pool (liquid) 10%, Newcastle University (vested) 1%, Researchers (vested) 5%, VitaDAO DFWG Members (vested) 5%.

content_blocks.title: Genesis Sale Structure
content_blocks.content: Fixed-price sale with pro rata distribution, overflow refunds, and 2-month vesting. 10% of VITA-FAST tokens sold to VITA holders at fixed price determined by cost-based valuation. Funds raised in wrapped ether (WETH) to minimize USD(C) inflation impact and minimize swaps.

content_blocks.title: Cost-Based Valuation
content_blocks.content: Price determined by cost-based valuation not factoring appreciation/depreciation based on emergent data. Price discovery occurs in open market once liquidity seeded. Cost calculation: VitaDAO committed research costs 220,358 CHF (245,557 USD) + incurred legal/operational costs 38,563 CHF (43,295 USD) + inflation 23,865 USD (calculated via US Bureau of Statistics Inflation Calculator) = total 312,717 USD for all 1,000,000 tokens. Therefore 10% valued at 31,271 USD = price of 0.31271 USD per VITA-FAST token.

content_blocks.title: Sale Mechanics - Bidding and Staking
content_blocks.content: Sale over 2-day period where VitaDAO members bid amount willing to contribute. To bid, must lock (stake) VITA equivalent to amount of ETH they want to bid. Buyer's bid can be up to maximum of their VITA holdings locked in sales contract. More VITA staked for 60-day vesting = more can bid during 2-day sale. If sales goal of 31,271 USD not met, all funds returned.

content_blocks.title: Sale Mechanics - Pro Rata Distribution and Overflow
content_blocks.content: If sales goal met or exceeded, final allocation proportional to each bidder's fraction of total bids. Example: Alice bids 40,000 USD, Bob bids 20,000 USD → Alice receives twice Bob's allocation (also staked twice as much VITA). Overflow refunds: excess amount above sales goal automatically returned via smart contract pro rata. Example: if 60,000 USD bid for 31,271 USD goal → surplus 28,279 USD returned pro rata.

content_blocks.title: Vesting and Token Unlock
content_blocks.content: Sale participants receive vested VITA (vVITA) and vested VITA-FAST (vVITA-FAST) to enable governance participation while vesting. After 2-month (60-day) vesting period, can swap vVITA for VITA and vVITA-FAST for VITA-FAST. Tokens locked for full 2-month vesting period.

content_blocks.title: VITA-FAST Molecules Crowdsale Flow Diagram
content_blocks.content: Visual flowchart showing: Sale period (2 days) → bidding with VITA staking → Vesting period (60 days) → pro rata token claim and refund → Unwrap tokens. Shows example flows for Bob (5 ETH + 5,000 VITA → 250 VITA-FAST + 4,000 vVITA, 1 ETH refund + 1,000 VITA) and Alice (15 ETH + 15,000 VITA → 750 VITA-FAST + 12,000 vVITA, 3 ETH refund + 3,000 VITA). Funding Goal: 10 ETH, VITA-FAST Price: 0.0016 ETH, VITA Price: 0.001 ETH, VITA-FAST for Sale: 1,000.

content_blocks.title: Use of Funds - Initial Application
content_blocks.content: Immediate application: establish liquidity for VITA-FAST. VITA-FAST holders have power to decide subsequent use of funds for advancing longevity research and IP development at Korolchuk lab.

content_blocks.title: Use of Funds - Potential Applications
content_blocks.content: Example uses decided by token holder governance: (1) Decentralization of Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) analysis - Korolchuk lab has computational biologist but objective is to harness superior SAR expertise from global pool via LabDAO network of computational chemists and hackathons to identify promising compound scaffolds and design optimal candidates for medicinal chemistry optimization, (2) Engage experienced medicinal chemists, (3) File patents - robust substance of matter patent protection for most promising compounds vital to enhance IP value given potential volume of novel compounds.

content_blocks.title: Risk Factors
content_blocks.content: List of potential risks (not exhaustive): Liquidity Risk (limited market for trading, may not resell easily), Regulatory Risk (evolving landscape could impact value/legality), Project Risk (research success not guaranteed, delays, cost overruns, unexpected results), Technological Risk (loss due to hacking, technical glitches, blockchain issues), Smart Contract Risk (bugs or security vulnerabilities), Market Risk (value fluctuation due to NFT/biotech IP/crypto market changes), Intellectual Property Risk (disputes or uncertainties regarding ownership/enforcement).

content_blocks.title: Risk Factors (continued)
content_blocks.content: Additional risks: Legal and Compliance Risk (jurisdiction-dependent legal implications including tax liabilities), Network Risk (Ethereum blockchain changes/issues like consensus mechanism changes, forks, network congestion), Operational Risk (platform/exchange downtime impacting ability to manage purchase), Governance Risk (disagreements or disputes among token holders).

content_blocks.title: Legal Considerations - Token Rights
content_blocks.content: VITA-FAST represents membership in IP pool containing Discovering Novel Autophagy Activators IP-NFT and attached IP/R&D data. Enables token holders to govern joint development and experimental use of IP/R&D data. Token holders may have rights to: (1) govern issuance of licenses to use underlying IP and R&D data (licensing function decentralized through token holder governance), (2) govern proceeds from licensing or sale of IP/R&D data (distribution not hard-coded; token holders must make careful future judgment about whether/how to distribute proceeds, such as forming legal entity prior to distribution; recommended to solicit legal advice).

content_blocks.title: Legal Considerations - Information Rights
content_blocks.content: Token holders may be granted information rights: regular non-confidential updates from researchers, ability to pose queries, access to data rooms containing IP/R&D data including continuously open non-confidential data room (aids community decision-making via research results with proprietary data redacted to safeguard IP integrity, primary means for delivering non-confidential updates). Token holders may also access confidential data subject to KYC and NDA protocols as governed by token holders (highly confidential data requiring adherence to protocols to safeguard IP).

content_blocks.title: Legal Considerations - Regulatory Compliance
content_blocks.content: VITA-FAST will not be sold to U.S. persons in compliance with Regulation S of United States Securities Act of 1933. Not available to U.S. persons or within United States. Potential purchasers required to provide sufficient proof of non-U.S. person status including IP address checks, geofencing, and attestations.

content_blocks.title: Team - Viktor Korolchuk
content_blocks.content: Cell biologist and researcher leading Korolchuk Lab at Newcastle University. PhD from University of Cambridge (2007). Postdoctoral positions at UC San Diego and University of Cambridge. Research focuses on molecular mechanisms regulating cellular homeostasis, particularly in neurodegenerative diseases and cancer. Numerous publications in high-impact journals, recognized expert in field. Industry collaborations and funding from UK Medical Research Council, European Research Council, Wellcome Trust. Member of Biochemical Society and Royal Society of Biology.

content_blocks.title: Team - Peter Banks
content_blocks.content: Trained as geneticist at Newcastle University. PhD studying role of cohesins in C. elegans at Newcastle University. Postdoc with David Lydall on chromatin modifications in budding yeast where he became interested in robotics in genetic research. Developed skills in high throughput screening using unique Centre for Integrated Systems Biology of Ageing and Nutrition setup. Appointed 2011 to run new facility at Newcastle University Faculty of Medical Sciences. Initially offered genome-wide yeast screens, expanded to mammalian siRNA screening (2012). Facility continued developing, recruited Adrian Blackburn, incorporated bacteria and alternative yeasts like Candida albicans.

content_blocks.title: Team - Jóhannes Reynisson
content_blocks.content: Drug discovery expert and research lead at VitaDAO overseeing drug discovery projects and leading collaborations with academic institutions and industry partners. PhD in chemistry from University of Copenhagen. Extensive pharmaceutical industry experience as medicinal chemist and project leader at Novartis and AstraZeneca. Also worked as molecular modeler at Institute of Cancer Research (London, UK) and project leader at University of Auckland (NZ). Authored 150+ peer-reviewed publications.

content_blocks.title: Additional Team Support and Resources
content_blocks.content: Project has support from entire VitaDAO community of contributors. Listed resources: Korolchuk Lab - VitaDAO, Discovering Novel Autophagy Activators - Molecule, Funding Science Through Blockchain Technology and Cryptocurrencies - Newcastle University, Newcastle fractionalization proposal to VitaDAO (VDP-100), VitaDAO snapshot proposal approving VDP-100, Korolchuk-Free Association of Molecules (FAM) Membership Agreement.

key_findings: SCREENING STATUS: Thousands of compounds screened from initial library of ~4000 bioactive molecules using three assay types: cell survival (Npc1-/- vs Atg5-/-), luciferase-p62 clearance, and Halo-GFP-LC3 traffic-light assays

LEAD IDENTIFICATION: Several compounds emerged with potential autophagic properties; lead series identified with high chemical variability and no similarity to pre-existing autophagy inducers (representing novel IP potential)
CURRENT STAGE: Screening second generation of lead series derivatives at time of publication; awaiting delivery of additional chemical series for screening

EXPERIMENTAL MODEL: Mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs) with Npc1-/- mutation (Niemann-Pick Type C model) subjected to metabolic stress as readout for autophagy function; Atg5-/- cells as negative control

DISEASE TARGET: Niemann-Pick Type C chosen as monogenetic orphan disease model with well-understood biology and shorter path to approval; potential expansion to Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases with lysosomal dysfunction
MECHANISM: Autophagy activators targeting lysosomal dysfunction to prevent accumulation of dysfunctional mitochondria, ROS, DNA damage, NAD+ depletion, and eventual apoptosis

NEXT STEPS: Once leads identified, requires drug translation work (pharmacokinetics, target deconvolution, SAR, scalability, manufacturing), then validation in patient-derived fibroblasts/iPSCs/Npc1-mutant mice and cats
IP STATUS: VitaDAO owns IP rights via Sponsored Research Agreement with Newcastle University; high possibility of novel IP given chemical diversity of hits

TEAM EXPERTISE: Led by Dr. Viktor Korolchuk (Cambridge PhD, recognized autophagy expert) with high-throughput screening facility at Newcastle University and drug discovery oversight by Jóhannes Reynisson
FUNDING: Initial costs 220,358 CHF research + 38,563 CHF legal/operational = 312,717 USD total valuation; seeking 31,271 USD through token sale for continued development and liquidity
